
  ┌────────────────────────────┬───────────────┬────────┐
  │            File            │ status: value │ Valid? │
  ├────────────────────────────┼───────────────┼────────┤
  │ plan-jk-crash.md           │ open          │ ✗      │
  ├────────────────────────────┼───────────────┼────────┤
  │ plan-tui-polish-cleanup.md │ active        │ ✗      │
  └────────────────────────────┴───────────────┴────────┘

  Both look like hand-edits (or imports from an older/foreign convention — jk-crash also uses
  title:/created: instead of the name:/created_at: the materializer writes). The DB still
  has the last good copy of each (both draft); the markdown edits just aren't being ingested
  while the status is unparseable. Fixing is a one-word edit in each file
  (open→draft/accepted, active→draft/accepted) — the next poll would clear it.

  conflict (1 plan) — both sides changed

  plan-done-crow-cleanup hit the db_changed and file_changed branch (sync.py:223-231): both
  the DB row's body and the markdown file diverged from the last materialized hash. Sync
  won't guess a winner — clobbering either side would lose work — so it parks it as conflict
  with reason "database and markdown changed since last materialization." Nothing syncs in
  either direction until it's resolved (re-save from whichever side is canonical, and the
  matching-hash path takes over).

  Net: the two parse_errors are benign frontmatter mismatches; the one conflict is the sync
  loop correctly refusing to auto-merge a genuine two-sided divergence. Nothing's broken in
  the sync code itself — it's doing exactly what it should given the file contents. Happy to
  fix any/all of them if you want, but leaving as-is per your ask.

✻ Baked for 1m 13s

❯ wondering if we arrived at this state becasue of changes to murder codebase and these are
  old files, or is the current pipeline capable of leading to wedged state like this?

● Good question — let me distinguish "stale artifacts" from "reachable today." Let me check
  the history and whether the current writer could ever produce these.

✢ Noodling… (7s · ↓ 327 tokens · thought for 5s)
  ⎿  Tip: Say "fan out subagents" and Claude sends a team. Each one digs deep so nothing gets
     missed.

❯

  ⏵⏵ bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle) · esc to interrupt
